note: The counterfactual, executed on-chain (anvil, audited contract). Two identical positions — 100 mEURC collateral, 50 USDC debt — drift toward liquidation. One has a Firebreak Mandate, one does not. Same drift, opposite outcomes.
scenario: mEURC LT 0.70 drifts 1.08 -> 0.85 (both cross Alice's 1.20 trigger) -> 0.55 (Bob crosses HF < 1.0)

protected.who: Alice — signed a Firebreak Mandate (DELEVERAGE allowed, trigger 1.20)
protected.action: keeper deleveraged at the trigger — sold 17 mEURC, repaid ~19 USDC of debt
protected.cost: an early, bounded deleverage (swap fee ~0.3%); position stays solvent

unprotected.outcome: rode the drift to HF 0.77 and was liquidated
unprotected.collateralSeized: all 100 of 100 mEURC — the liquidator seized 110% of the debt at the crashed price, taking the entire position to cover 50 USDC of debt
unprotected.loss: the whole leveraged position, plus the 10% liquidation penalty

takeaway: Same starting position, same market. The firewall acted early at HF 1.20 and kept 83% of the collateral solvent; the unprotected twin lost 100% of it to a liquidation. Firebreak turns liquidation from the default outcome into the avoidable one.
